hamiPage.title: Was ist
hamiPage.subtitle: An open-source, heterogeneous AI computing virtualization middleware, scales AI workloads seamlessly across hybrid and multi-cloud environments.
hamiPage.introduction: HAMi is the only open-source project dedicated to GPU resource sharing. It maximizing GPU efficiency by flexible, reliable, and elastic GPU share, integrating seamlessly into hybrid AI infrastructure. Adopted by 60+ OS vendors, cloud providers, and industry leaders, it has enabled breakthroughs in finance, logistics, autonomous driving, robotics, and biotech.
hamiPage.creator: HAMi was created by dynamia.ai and is a Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) sandbox project.
hamiPage.solutionTitle: One-Stop Heterogeneous AI Computing Management Solution
hamiPage.solutionDesc: HAMi is a one-stop middleware that enables the sharing of various AI devices while ensuring resource isolation between different tasks. By improving the utilization of heterogeneous computing devices, HAMi provides a unified reuse interface to meet the needs of different device types.

0.title: Kubernetes Native API Compatibility
0.description: One of HAMi's standout features is its compatibility with Kubernetes native APIs. This means users can upgrade to HAMi without modifying existing configurations, allowing for a seamless transition while maintaining Kubernetes default behaviors.

1.title: Open and Neutral
1.description: HAMi is a collaborative initiative involving stakeholders from various domains, including internet services, finance, manufacturing, and cloud service providers. The goal is to establish open governance under the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), ensuring HAMi remains neutral and accessible to all users.

2.title: Avoid Vendor Lock-in
2.description: With HAMi, users can integrate with mainstream cloud service providers without being tied to proprietary vendor orchestrations. This flexibility allows organizations to choose their preferred cloud solution while leveraging HAMi's capabilities.

3.title: Resource Isolation
3.description: HAMi provides robust resource isolation within containers. Each task running in a container is confined to its allocated resource boundaries, preventing any task from exceeding its quota. This strict isolation enhances security and stability in the computing environment.

4.title: Support for Multiple Heterogeneous Computing Devices
4.description: HAMi excels in supporting various heterogeneous computing devices. Whether it's GPUs, MLUs, or NPUs from different manufacturers, HAMi facilitates device sharing and maximizes resource efficiency across different hardware platforms.

5.title: Unified Management
5.description: To simplify operations, HAMi offers a unified monitoring system, along with configurable scheduling policies such as binpacking and spreading. This comprehensive management approach streamlines the governance of resources and enhances overall system performance.

sfTechnologyEffectiveGpu.subtitle: Discover how SF Technology built EffectiveGPU based on the open-source HAMi framework, deeply integrating heterogeneous computing virtualization and efficient scheduling capabilities to achieve production deployment in key scenarios like AI large model inference and voice recognition, significantly improving GPU utilization and realizing cost reduction and efficiency enhancement while promoting HAMi open-source ecosystem development.

overview.title: Unternehmensuebersicht
overview.description: SF Technology is the technology arm of SF Express, one of China's leading logistics companies. As a technology-driven enterprise, SF Technology focuses on developing innovative solutions for logistics, AI, and cloud computing services.

challenge.title: Traditional GPU Management Challenges
challenge.subtitle: Core Pain Points
challenge.description: Traditional GPU usage patterns (such as whole-card exclusive allocation) led to GPUs being underutilized in inference and other light-load scenarios, resulting in serious resource waste.

points.0: Low Resource Utilization: GPU average utilization remained below 30% for extended periods, with particularly pronounced idle computing power and memory issues in inference and testing scenarios.
points.1: Coarse Scheduling Granularity: Lack of fine-grained resource partitioning and sharing capabilities, making it difficult to achieve multi-task concurrency and resource reuse.
points.2: Heterogeneous Adaptation Difficulties: Mixed deployment of GPUs, NPUs, domestic AI chips and other multi-type devices posed ecosystem fragmentation and management complexity challenges for scheduling systems.
points.3: Impact on ROI: These issues directly affected the deployment flexibility of AI services and the return on investment (ROI) of computing infrastructure.

solution.title: Breaking Through with EffectiveGPU Technology Practice
solution.subtitle: Innovation Based on HAMi
solution.description: Facing these challenges, SF Technology's team launched the EffectiveGPU technology solution based on the open-source heterogeneous computing scheduling framework HAMi, combined with their own business scenario requirements.
solution.objective: The goal is to build an efficient, flexible, and unified GPU resource pooling and scheduling management system to solve problems of low resource utilization and management complexity.

0.title: GPU-Pooling und Virtualisierung
0.description: Integrated scattered GPU resources into a unified resource pool, enabling on-demand resource allocation through virtualization technology. This capability is based on and extends HAMi's virtualization foundation.

1.title: Feingranulare Ressourcenpartitionierung
1.description: Supports precise partitioning by core utilization (computing power) and memory capacity, allowing a single GPU card to serve multiple applications with different requirements simultaneously, breaking the limitation of whole-card exclusive allocation. This benefits from HAMi's flexible partitioning mechanism.

2.title: Elastische Ressourcenueberbuchung
2.description: Introduced dual-dimension overcommitment technology for memory and computing power (up to 200% memory overcommitment ratio), combined with priority scheduling to further exploit GPU potential while ensuring QoS for high-priority tasks.

3.title: Einheitliche Verwaltung und Planung
3.description: Provides unified scheduling interfaces, abstracting and shielding underlying hardware differences, supporting unified management and efficient scheduling of heterogeneous resources including domestic GPUs. This aligns with HAMi's vision of building a unified abstraction driver framework.

results.title: Significant Results: Substantial Improvement in Resource Utilization and Cost Reduction
results.description: The solution has completed multi-scenario deployment on SF Technology's AI platform, achieving remarkable results.

0.title: Inferenzdienste fuer grosse Modelle
0.description: 65 Dienste mit 28 GPU-Karten bereitgestellt, 37 Karten eingespart

1.title: Test-Service-Cluster
1.description: 19 Dienste mit 6 Test-GPUs bereitgestellt, 13 Karten eingespart

2.title: Spracherkennungsdienste
2.description: Echtzeitleistung fuer kritische Aufgaben durch Prioritaetsplanung und Ressourcenueberbuchung

3.title: Anpassung inlaendischer Rechenhardware
3.description: Successfully adapted Ascend, Kunlun and other domestic AI chips with complete scheduling support, with compatibility partially benefiting from HAMi's heterogeneous compatibility design

4.title: Leistungseinfluss
4.description: Minimale Leistungsverschlechterung von nur 0,5% nach Hinzufuegen der Pooling-Schicht

hamiIntegration.title: Tiefe Integration mit dem HAMi-Oekosystem, Aufbau effizienter Computing-Infrastruktur
hamiIntegration.description: Der erfolgreiche Einsatz der EffectiveGPU-Technologie ist untrennbar mit der tiefen Integration in das Open-Source-Framework HAMi verbunden.

points.0: Die technische Architektur von EffectiveGPU integriert die Kernfaehigkeiten von HAMi in den Bereichen heterogene Computing-Virtualisierung und effiziente Planung.
points.1: Insbesondere bei der Verwaltung und Planung inlaendischer GPUs wurden durch die Integration des HAMi-Oekosystems einheitliche Abstraktionstreiber-Frameworks entwickelt.
points.2: Die EffectiveGPU-Loesung verwendet HAMi-oekosystemkompatible Designs bei Virtualisierungsschnittstellen und Planungsschnittstellen.

validation.title: Validierung des HAMi-Werts, Foerderung der Reife heterogener Computing-Planung
validation.description: Der erfolgreiche EffectiveGPU-Einsatz von SF Technology ist eine weitere ueberzeugende Validierung der technischen Konzepte von HAMi.

points.0: Beweist, dass die Schluesselfaehigkeiten von HAMi die tatsaechlichen Anforderungen grosser Unternehmen vollstaendig unterstuetzen koennen.
points.1: Als CNCF Sandbox & CNAI Landscape-Projekt hat diese Praxis wertvolle Erfahrungen fuer HAMi gesammelt.

quote.text: Durch die enge Zusammenarbeit mit der HAMi-Open-Source-Community hat EffectiveGPU uns geholfen, die GPU-Ressourceneffizienz erheblich zu verbessern.
quote.author: SF Technology AI Platform Lead

conclusion.title: Zukunftsausblick
conclusion.description: SF Technology hat die Kernprobleme des GPU-Ressourcenmanagements durch die EffectiveGPU-Loesung auf Basis von HAMi effektiv geloest.

prepEduHami.title: PREP EDU × HAMi | Intelligent Heterogeneous GPU Scheduling for Enhanced AI Service Efficiency
prepEduHami.subtitle: PREP EDU deployed the HAMi framework to add fine-grained vGPU sharing and GPU aware scheduling to its production Kubernetes (RKE2) clusters. This addressed concrete problems: GPU utilization below 20%, frequent out-of-memory crashes from shared cards, and the operational overhead of manually scheduling workloads across mixed GPU hardware. The result was a measurable increase in inference task density and platform stability.

overview.title: Unternehmensuebersicht
overview.description: PREP EDU is one of Southeast Asia’s fastest-growing edtech companies. challenge.title: Rising Complexity in Heterogeneous GPU Scheduling
challenge.description: As PREP EDU’s large-scale AI inference workloads expanded, traditional GPU usage models could no longer support the rapidly growing service demands. In a mixed-GPU environment (RTX 4070 / RTX 4090), resource waste, unbalanced scheduling, and compatibility issues became critical.

points.0: Low GPU Utilization: Allocating GPUs exclusively as full cards prevented inference workloads from making effective use of available resources. Average utilization often remained as low as 10–20%, leaving both compute capacity and memory significantly underused.
points.1: Frequent Resource Conflicts: Without proper isolation and scheduling mechanisms, competing workloads frequently triggered memory contention, pushing GPU memory usage to 90–95%. This led to application crashes, interrupted inference processes, and ultimately impacted overall service stability.
points.2: Challenges in Heterogeneous Scheduling: In mixed-GPU environments combining RTX 4070 and 4090 models, different projects often required specific GPU types. Lacking a unified allocation and selection mechanism, resource dispatching became complex and error-prone.
points.3: High Compatibility Barriers: Any new solution needed to remain fully compatible with existing components such as RKE2, GPU Operator, and containerd. Non-transparent or intrusive approaches risked increasing operational overhead or disrupting existing production workflows.

solution.title: Solution: Implementing Efficient GPU Orchestration with HAMi
solution.description: To address long-standing issues such as heterogeneous scheduling complexity, low GPU utilization, and insufficient resource isolation, PREP EDU adopted HAMi to build a lightweight and non-intrusive GPU virtualization and orchestration solution within its existing RKE2 + GPU Operator + multi-model NVIDIA GPU environment.
solution.objective: Through this integration, PREP EDU significantly reduced resource waste and conflict frequency while ensuring the platform can scale smoothly with growing AI inference workloads.

0.title: Virtualisierung & GPU-Partitionierung
0.description: Workloads received resource limits based on NLP token lengths and service needs, enabling precise vGPU allocation.

1.title: Heterogene GPU-Verwaltung
1.description: With HAMi, workloads can be scheduled by GPU type (e.g., run specific services only on RTX 4070 or 4090), using annotations to ensure compatibility and performance.

2.title: Nahtlose Anwendungsintegration
2.description: Transparent device virtualization allows GPU sharing and isolation without modifying existing applications.

3.title: GPU-spezifische Zuweisung
3.description: Tasks can be allocated by GPU UUID, enabling multiple processes to run on a single 24GB RTX 4090 in a controlled manner.

4.title: Vollstaendige Kompatibilitaet
4.description: HAMi and NVIDIA GPU Operator coexist smoothly, both running on containerd. Combined with Prometheus monitoring, the system integrates seamlessly with RKE2 and containerd.

daoCloud.title: Building Flexible GPU Clouds with HAMi at DaoCloud
daoCloud.subtitle: Discover how DaoCloud operates two major cloud native platforms for AI workloads—D.run Compute Cloud and DaoCloud Enterprise—using HAMi to achieve >80% GPU utilization across 10,000+ GPUs spanning 10+ data centers.

D.run Compute Cloud is a public GPU cloud serving individual developers and small teams, while DaoCloud Enterprise (DCE) is a private Kubernetes platform for enterprise customers running both training and inference.

keyPoints.1: D.run Compute Cloud fuer oeffentliche GPU-Cloud

0.title: Ganzkarten-Zuweisung
0.description: Many inference and lightweight workloads used only a fraction of GPU resources, leaving significant portions of compute and memory underutilized and limiting how DaoCloud could package GPU SKUs.

1.title: Heterogene Hardware

nio.title: NIO Improves GPU Utilization for Autonomous Driving Workloads with HAMi
nio.subtitle: Discover how NIO adopted a hybrid GPU sharing strategy using HAMi to achieve 10× GPU utilization improvement in CI pipelines and 30% reduction in GPU hours for simulation workloads across 600 GPUs supporting autonomous driving AI infrastructure.

overview.title: Unternehmensuebersicht
overview.description: NIO operates large-scale cloud infrastructure to support autonomous driving workloads, including model training, simulation, CI/testing, and online inference. The team focuses on GPU performance optimization and participates in GPU and compute resource planning decisions for their comprehensive autonomous driving platform.

keyPoints.0: Gross angelegter GPU-Cluster: 600 GPUs auf ~80 Knoten
keyPoints.1: Vielfaeltige autonome Fahrworkloads: Training, Simulation, CI/Test, Inferenz

challenge.title: Challenge: Low GPU Utilization Across Diverse Workloads
challenge.description: NIO's large-scale cloud infrastructure supports diverse autonomous driving workloads. This diversity led to persistent efficiency challenges due to workload-resource mismatch, with several specific pain points.

0.title: CI- und Testaufgaben
0.description: Most execution time for CI tasks spent on CPU-intensive operations (compilation, file fetching, preprocessing). GPUs used intermittently with only 5–10% average utilization under full-GPU allocation.

1.title: Simulations-Workloads
1.description: Simulation pipelines process video streams, radar data, and inference validation. Individual tasks have relatively low compute requirements, making them suitable for concurrent execution on shared GPUs.

2.title: Online-Inferenz
2.description: Many inference services require only ¼ or ½ of a GPU. Allocating an entire GPU is both inefficient and costly for these small workloads.

3.title: Begrenzter GPU-Cluster
3.description: The GPU cluster size is limited, with part of capacity sourced from public cloud providers with time-based billing. Low GPU utilization wastes GPU hours and increases operational costs.

solution.title: Hybride GPU-Sharing-Strategie mit HAMi
solution.description: NIO adopted a hybrid GPU sharing strategy, selecting different GPU allocation mechanisms based on workload characteristics rather than enforcing a single approach. HAMi extends Kubernetes with fine-grained GPU sharing capabilities through scheduler extensions and device plugin integration.

0.title: NVIDIA MIG
0.description: Provides strong isolation but supports only predefined partition sizes, making it difficult to match finer-grained requirements (e.g., 1/6 or 1/8 of a GPU).

1.title: Time-Slicing
1.description: Allows workloads to compete freely for GPU resources with minimal overhead. However, it lacks strict limits on memory and compute usage, making it unsuitable for certain production workloads.

2.title: HAMi (CNCF Sandbox)
2.description: Supports fine-grained control over both GPU memory and compute allocation, enabling proportional allocation based on actual workload requirements with minimal overhead.

productionStrategy.title: Production Strategy: Combining Multiple Approaches
productionStrategy.description: Rather than replacing existing mechanisms, NIO combined them for optimal efficiency across different workload types.

items.0: MIG: Verwendet fuer Algorithmenentwicklung und Umgebungen mit strenger Isolierung
items.1: HAMi: Verwendet fuer CI-Aufgaben und ausgewaehlte Inferenz- und Simulations-Workloads
items.2: Time-Slicing: Verwendet fuer Workloads, die Ressourcenkonflikte tolerieren koennen

implementation.title: Implementation Details
implementation.scale: HAMi deployed on approximately 50–70 active nodes with 400–560 GPUs

resourceDesign.title: Resource Allocation Design
resourceDesign.description: For simulation workloads, NIO treated GPU memory (VRAM) as the primary constraint since insufficient compute capacity can be mitigated through scheduling delay, but memory exhaustion immediately triggers OOM failure.
resourceDesign.insight: Finer partitioning is not always better. For certain simulation workloads, allocating approximately 1/6 of a GPU provided optimal efficiency. Smaller fractions (such as 1/8) introduced additional scheduling and virtualization overhead, reducing overall throughput.

results.title: Impact: Dramatic Efficiency Gains
results.description: The hybrid GPU sharing strategy delivered measurable improvements across multiple workload types, increasing overall system throughput while directly reducing GPU costs.

0.title: CI-Workload-Auslastung
0.description: By partitioning GPUs into ¼ or smaller fractions, effective GPU utilization in CI pipelines increased by approximately 4×

1.title: Simulations-GPU-Stunden
1.description: Feingranulares GPU-Sharing reduzierte die GPU-Stunden um ca. 30%

2.title: Simulations-Aufgabendauer
2.description: End-to-End-Simulationsaufgaben von ca. 3 auf ca. 2 Tage reduziert

5.title: Strategie
5.description: HAMi + MIG + Time-Slicing fuer optimale Ergebnisse

lessonsLearned.title: Erkenntnisse
lessonsLearned.description: NIO's deployment of HAMi provided valuable insights for organizations implementing GPU virtualization at scale.

0.title: GPU-Partitionierungsoptimierung
0.description: GPU partitioning is not 'the finer, the better.' Each workload has an optimal partition size. Over-fragmentation can reduce efficiency.

1.title: Leistungsvalidierung
1.description: Version upgrades require performance validation. New GPU virtualization components may introduce performance regressions. NIO adopted a phased upgrade strategy, validating performance benchmarks before allowing different HAMi components to run at different versions.

2.title: Produktionssicherheit
2.description: Operational changes must ensure production safety. For online inference workloads, device plugin upgrades follow a blue–green deployment–like process: traffic is migrated first, new Pods are deployed, and old instances are gradually decommissioned.

3.title: Toolchain-Kompatibilitaet
3.description: Toolchain compatibility is critical. Certain compiler or library features (such as pointer or address analysis) may conflict with GPU interception mechanisms. Careful trade-offs between functionality and stability are required.

quote.text: NIO's deployment of HAMi demonstrates how fine-grained GPU sharing can significantly improve infrastructure efficiency for autonomous driving workloads. By combining HAMi with Kubernetes and existing GPU allocation mechanisms such as MIG and time-slicing, NIO increased GPU utilization, reduced overall GPU hours, and improved workload throughput without compromising stability.
quote.author: NIO Infrastructure Team

companyCard.name: NIO
companyCard.description: Leading electric vehicle company with autonomous driving AI platform

conclusion.title: Hybrid Strategy Success
conclusion.description: NIO's hybrid resource management strategy enables the company to support diverse AI workloads—from CI pipelines to simulation and inference—more efficiently within the same Kubernetes environment. This approach demonstrates how combining HAMi with other GPU allocation mechanisms can deliver optimal results for different workload types.

snowCorp.title: SNOW Corp Scales GenAI for 200M Users with HAMi GPU Sharing and KEDA Autoscaling
snowCorp.subtitle: Discover how SNOW Corp orchestrates 1,000+ GPUs to handle 700% viral traffic spikes, achieving 91% MTTR reduction, 85% fewer surge errors, and USD 17.4M in estimated cost savings using HAMi and KEDA on Kubernetes.

overview.title: Unternehmensuebersicht
overview.description: SNOW Corp., a subsidiary of NAVER from South Korea, operates a fleet of 1,000+ A100 GPUs serving GenAI features for 200M+ global users across three top-ranked apps (SNOW, EPIK, B612). The infrastructure serves 1,200+ AI workflows and 400+ models, handling extreme traffic volatility from viral AI trends.

keyPoints.0: Tochter von NAVER, Suedkoreas fuehrende Tech-Plattform

challenge.title: Challenge: GPU Scheduling at Extreme Scale
challenge.description: Kubernetes' native GPU scheduling treats GPUs as atomic resources — a pod either gets a full GPU or nothing. This model broke down under SNOW's heterogeneous workload demands and unpredictable viral traffic spikes.

0.title: Heterogene Workload-Anforderungen
0.description: Training pipelines, inference services, and batch processing have vastly different GPU utilization profiles. A training job might consume 80% of GPU compute but only 20% of memory, while inference has the opposite profile.

1.title: Traffic-Unvorhersehbarkeit
1.description: Viral AI trends and heterogeneous inference workflows created 700% traffic spikes. Without GPU-level observability, scaling decisions were either manual or based on crude CPU/memory metrics that don't reflect GPU saturation.

2.title: Planungsblindheit
2.description: Before Kubernetes adoption, 2-3 containers in the same pod competed for GPU resources with no coordination. Manual GPU provisioning based on load predictions led to ~2x over-provisioning.

3.title: Kostenexplosion
3.description: To handle peak loads, SNOW had to over-provision by approximately 2x — one GPU for training, one for inference — when one could theoretically suffice.

solution.title: Solution: CNCF-Powered GPU Orchestration Stack
solution.description: SNOW migrated to a multi-region on-premise Kubernetes platform with the CNCF ecosystem underpinning the entire stack: Cilium for CNI, Helm for GitOps-based deployment, Traefik for ingress, Prometheus/Loki/Grafana for observability, HAMi for GPU sharing, and KEDA for autoscaling.

hamiSharing.title: GPU Sharing with HAMi
hamiSharing.description: Kubernetes' default scheduler enforces strict GPU isolation, which blocked migration of SNOW's sequential Train-to-Inference pipelines — where a trainer and inference engine must share a single GPU. HAMi resolves this by virtualizing GPU resources (vGPU), enabling multiple containers within the same pod to share a single GPU concurrently.

features.0: Integriert sich nativ mit kube-scheduler ohne Anwendungscode-Aenderungen
features.1: Vollstaendig kompatibel mit dem breiteren Autoskalierungs-Oekosystem
features.2: Ermoeglicht 2x weniger GPUs fuer Training + Inferenz

kedaAutoscaling.title: Proactive GPU Orchestration with KEDA
kedaAutoscaling.description: Standard metrics (CPU/RAM, DCGM utilization) proved unreliable for SNOW's heterogeneous workloads. KEDA's built-in RabbitMQ scaler functioned as a lagging indicator — given a ~60-second model warm-up time, scaling triggered after a queue backlog formed was consistently too late.

features.0: Custom Metric Server (Python/FastAPI) stellt Consumer-Saettigungsmetrik fuer KEDA bereit
features.1: Proactive scaling: when active_ratio exceeds threshold (e.g., 0.7), KEDA provisions new GPU pods before worker pool saturates
features.2: Intelligentes Scale-In: laengere Stabilisierungsfenster verhindern vorzeitige Freigabe

cloudBursting.title: Hybrid Cloud Bursting for Viral Spikes
cloudBursting.description: When viral trends like the 'Ghibli Filter' tripled traffic within 3 hours, SNOW expanded dynamically into CSP regions using a unified GitOps pipeline that deployed identical Helm charts across all clusters.

features.0: Einheitliche GitOps-Pipeline mit identischen Helm-Charts ueber alle Cluster
features.1: CSP-Workerknoten konsumierten Aufgaben direkt aus zentraler RabbitMQ-Warteschlange

results.title: Impact and Results
results.description: SNOW's cloud-native transformation demonstrates how combining Kubernetes with the broader CNCF ecosystem can overcome fundamental limitations in GPU scheduling and observability at extreme scale.

0.title: MTTR-Verbesserung
0.description: Von ~2 Std. auf ~10 Min. reduziert

1.title: Reduzierung von Spitzenfehlern
1.description: GPU-spitzenbezogene Benutzerfehler waehrend Spitzenverkehr

2.title: Kosteneinsparungen
2.description: Geschaetzte Einsparungen vs. On-Demand Cloud GPU-Bereitstellung

3.title: GPU-Nutzungszeit
3.description: Durchschnittliche GPU-Nutzungszeit durch proaktive Autoskalierung reduziert

4.title: GPU-Bedarf
4.description: GPUs fuer Training + Inferenz durch HAMi-Sharing benoetigt

5.title: Betriebseinsparungen
5.description: Manuelle Skalierung eliminiert, metrikgesteuerte Automatisierung eingefuehrt

cncfNote.title: CNCF Case Study
cncfNote.description: This case study was originally published by the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) as part of its end-user case study program. It demonstrates SNOW Corp.'s success with HAMi, a CNCF Sandbox project, within the broader CNCF ecosystem.

quote.text: SNOW's cloud-native transformation demonstrates how combining Kubernetes with the broader CNCF ecosystem can overcome fundamental limitations in GPU scheduling and observability at extreme scale. By introducing HAMi for GPU sharing and augmenting KEDA with proactive, custom metrics, SNOW shifted from reactive, manual operations to predictive, automated orchestration.
quote.author: SNOW Corp. Infrastructure Team

companyCard.name: SNOW Corp.

conclusion.description: SNOW's approach highlights a replicable blueprint for running large-scale GenAI workloads — where intelligent resource utilization, autoscaling precision, and deep observability are critical to turning infrastructure challenges into competitive advantage.

cookiesPolicy.title: Cookie-Richtlinie
cookiesPolicy.intro:
This Cookies Policy explains how DYNAMIA INTELLIGENCE PTE. LTD. uses cookies and similar technologies on https://dynamia.ai/.

We use necessary cookies to operate the Site. Optional categories, including analytics, marketing, and functional cookies, are disabled by default and are used only after you provide consent through our cookie banner or preferences panel.

You can change your choices at any time by opening Cookie Settings in the footer.

0.title: Was sind Cookies?
0.content:
Cookies are small text files consisting solely of letters and numbers. A web server places them on your computer or mobile device when you visit a webpage. Cookies can enhance the user - friendliness of our Services, for instance, by remembering your language preferences and settings. For more details about cookies, visit www.allaboutcookies.org.

Cookies are extensively used to make websites function efficiently. They allow you to navigate between pages smoothly, remember your preferences, and make your interaction with the Services more seamless and efficient. Cookies also help ensure that the online advertisements you see are relevant to you and your interests.

1.title: Cookie-Kategorien
1.content:
Necessary: required for core site behavior such as language routing, remembering your cookie consent choice, and maintaining limited access state for gated video content.

Analytics: used only with consent to understand traffic and performance through Google Analytics, Vercel Analytics, and Vercel Speed Insights.

Marketing: reserved for campaign measurement, advertising, or remarketing. We do not enable marketing cookies unless you opt in.

Functional: reserved for optional experience preferences beyond what is strictly required. We do not enable functional cookies unless you opt in.

2.title: Aktuelle Cookies und Technologien
2.content:
dynamia_cookie_consent: a first-party necessary cookie that stores your consent record, including version, timestamp, and enabled categories. It is retained for up to 180 days.

NEXT_LOCALE: a first-party necessary cookie used to remember language preference. It is retained for up to one year.

video_unlocked: a first-party necessary cookie used to remember that video content has been unlocked on this browser. It is retained for up to 30 days.

Google Analytics (_ga and related cookies): third-party analytics cookies used only after analytics consent. Retention is controlled by Google Analytics settings.

Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights: analytics and performance measurement technologies used only after analytics consent. Retention is controlled by Vercel.

3.title: Ihre Wahlmoeglichkeiten
3.content:
You can accept all optional categories, reject all optional categories, or manage individual categories through our cookie banner. You can change your decision later by selecting Cookie Settings in the footer.

You may also restrict or block cookies through your browser settings. Blocking necessary cookies may affect site behavior.

4.title: Einwilligungsaufzeichnungen
4.content: When you save your choice, we store a consent record in your browser. The record includes the policy version, timestamp, and whether analytics, marketing, and functional categories are enabled. Necessary cookies remain enabled because they are required for the Site to operate.

5.title: Aenderungen unserer Cookie-Richtlinie
5.content: We may update our Cookie Policy from time to time. Changes to our Cookie Policy will be updated on this page. We encourage users to frequently check this page for any changes. The date of the last update to our Cookie Policy is indicated at the top of the policy.

faq.title: Haeufig gestellte Fragen
faq.subtitle: Finden Sie Antworten auf haeufige Fragen zu Dynamia AI und unserer heterogenen Computing-Plattform.

questions.question: Was ist Dynamia AI?
questions.answer: Dynamia AI ist eine heterogene Computing-Plattform fuer Unternehmen, die auf HAMi-Technologie basiert. Sie bietet GPU-Sharing, Auto-Scaling und einheitliches Management fuer KI-Workloads.

questions.question: Was ist HAMi?
questions.answer: HAMi (Heterogeneous AI Computing Middleware) ist ein Open-Source-Projekt und CNCF Sandbox-Projekt, das GPU-Virtualisierung und Ressourcenverwaltung bietet. Es ist die Kerntechnologie hinter Dynamia AI.

questions.question: Wie funktioniert GPU-Sharing?
questions.answer: Dynamia AI konsolidiert und orchestriert GPU-Ressourcen dynamisch, sodass mehrere Workloads GPU-Speicher und Rechenleistung effizient teilen koennen.

questions.question: Welche Workloads unterstuetzt Dynamia AI?
questions.answer: Dynamia AI unterstuetzt KI/ML-Training und -Inferenz, High-Performance Computing (HPC), Edge Computing und verschiedene andere rechenintensive Workloads.

questions.question: Ist Dynamia AI mit Kubernetes kompatibel?
questions.answer: Ja, Dynamia AI ist fuer Kubernetes-Umgebungen entwickelt und laesst sich nahtlos in bestehende Kubernetes-Cluster integrieren.

questions.question: Wie werden die Preise bestimmt?
questions.answer: Dynamia AI bietet flexible Preise basierend auf Cluster-Groesse, Knotenanzahl und GPU-Anzahl. Wir bieten massgeschneiderte Plaene, die mit Ihrer Infrastruktur skalieren.

questions.question: Welcher Support ist enthalten?
questions.answer: Enterprise-Kunden erhalten professionellen technischen Support, einschliesslich Einrichtungshilfe, Fehlerbehebung, Best-Practice-Beratung und kontinuierliche Updates.

questions.question: Kann ich Dynamia AI vor dem Kauf testen?
questions.answer: Ja, wir bieten kostenlose Testversionen fuer qualifizierte Organisationen an. Sie koennen ueber unsere Website eine Testversion beantragen.
